data.title: Match Group provides new forecast, dividend, and buyback plan; shares down
U.S. federal budget shortfall widens further in November
data.description: The U.S. government spent $367.0B more than it earned in November, wider than the $349.0B deficit expected and the $257.0B shortfall in October, according to the Treasury Department's statement issued on Wednesday.

data.title: AstraZeneca, Merck report positive long-term data for Lynparza
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) and Merck (NYSE:MRK) said long-term data from a Phase 3 study showed that 87.5% of patients treated with their breast cancer drug Lynparza were still alive six years later, versus 83.2% who were given a placebo.

The drug is being evaluated in patients with gBRCAm, HER2-negative high-risk early breast cancer.

The results were presented Wednesday at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium, according to a statement.

Lynparza, which is co-marketed by Merck (MRK) and AstraZeneca (AZN), is approved in the United States for the treatment of gBRCAm, HER2-negative high-risk early and metastatic breast cancer.

The drug is also approved for the treatment of certain types of ovarian, fallopian tube, primary peritoneal, prostate and pancreatic cancers that involve a BRCA gene mutation.

data.title: Dave & Buster's adds to losses as downgrades follow CEO departure, Q3 results
data.description:
Dave & Buster’s (NASDAQ:PLAY) disappointing Q3 results which included mounting losses and declining sales, coupled with the CEO’s exit, continues to drag down shares with PLAY sliding to its lowest level since September and leaving a sizable downside gap at the open.

The CEO departure “muddles” the turnaround story and “elevates execution risk,” says Truist’s Jake Bartlett, making Morris’s early exit all the more disappointing. Bartlett asked interim CEO Kevin Sheehan during the company’s earnings call how Chris Morris’s departure would impact the turnaround plan, and despite assurances the plan remains intact, there were few details that mitigated Bartlett’s decision to downgrade Dave & Buster’s (PLAY) to Hold from Buy and shaving 35% off the target price, moving to the sidelines until there is evidence of its turnaround.

William Blair is also less convinced of the likelihood for a steady improvement in comparable store sales trends in 2025, and downgraded the stock to Market Perform from Outperform. The analyst team led by Sharon Zackfia cite the uncertainty related to the timing and benefit of remodels, the leadership transition, and marketing strategy for the lack of conviction, calling out increased competition, higher labor costs, and low frequency of customer visits as further risks to the downside.

The company has rolled out several initiatives that are already gaining traction, including higher chip prices, new games, and improved food and beverages. But Sheehan acknowledged that he’s not seeing “the impact of all these green shoots poke through the macro environment.” The media mix has also required some correction. After shifting to a 90% digital, 10% off-line, the company is now adding back some TV marketing to “drive top-of the-funnel awareness” as the pivot to digital was “overdone”.

With a heavy investment in remodeling restaurants, free cash flow will remain constrained and could likely necessitate more debt, says Seeking Alpha analyst Pedro Goulart, likely exacerbated by the need for further promotions to drive traffic. A tactic employed by Pinstripes (PNST) to drive awareness and sales at all its entertainment venues, for “improved profitability as the macro environment improves.”

Topgolf Callaway Brands (MODG) also cited the weaker consumer environment post-COVID for its lowered revenue guidance for FY24.

“I see a steadily weaker consumer environment where the consumer discretionary is increasingly under pressure, and clearly Topgolf is being impacted by that,” CEO Oliver Brewer said on the company’s earnings call last month.

Dave & Buster’s (PLAY) CEO concedes the economy continues to be a headwind. “That low-end consumer is really where we’re feeling that decline, to the extent that their spend is down twice as much as the other income quantiles.”

Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (PLAY) shares are down more than 16%.

data.title: Former TD Bank employee arrested for allegedly aiding in money laundering
data.description:
A former employee of a TD Bank (NYSE:TD) branch in Florida was arrested and criminally charged with facilitating money laundering to Colombia, the U.S. Department of Justice said Wednesday.

According to the complaint, the former employee had worked at a TD Bank store in Doral, Florida, between February and November 2023. As alleged, after another TD Bank employee opened accounts in names of shell companies with nominee owners, the suspect assisted with the money laundering by issuing dozens of debit cards for the accounts in exchange for bribes. Those accounts were then allegedly used to launder millions of dollars in narcotics proceeds through cash withdrawals at ATMs in Colombia, the Justice Department said.

The former employee was charged with one count of conspiracy to commit money laundering. If convicted, he'll face up to 20 years in prison.

An investigation into problems with TD Bank's (NYSE:TD) anti-money laundering controls led to the collapse of its agreement to acquire First Horizon (FHN) in 2023. In October 2023, TD Bank Group agreed to pay US$3.09B to settle investigations into its AML practices. In addition, an asset cap of US$434B was placed on the total assets of the bank's two U.S. banking subsidiaries — TD Bank, NA, and TD Bank USA, NA.

The Wall Street Journal had also reported that a federal investigation found that Chinese criminal groups had laundered millions of dollars of illegal fentanyl sales through that bank's U.S. branches.

data.title: Catalent gains after report sale to Novo Holdings could close soon
data.description:
Catalent (NYSE:CTLT) quickly rose 1% after a report that the contract drug manufacturer's $16.5 billion sale to Novo Holdings could close as soon as Monday.

The US Federal Trade Commission doesn't appear to have concerns about the acquisition at this point, according to traders, who cited a Capitol Forum report that circulated on Wednesday.

The news comes after the deal won approval from the European Commission on Friday. The companies said that the acquisition is expected to close towards the end of calendar year 2024.

Novo and Catalent (CTLT) are awaiting approval from the FTC. Catalent and Novo Nordisk Foundation received a request for more information from the FTC in May. There was a report last Tuesday that Novo was working on contract extensions with some drugmakers who are concerned about the Catalent sale in an effort to appease regulators.

In February, Catalent agreed to be acquired by Novo Holdings for $63.50 a share. Novo Holdings will then sell three Catalent manufacturing sites to Novo Nordisk (NVO), which should help increase production of the company’s hugely popular weight-loss drug, Wegovy.

data.title: Benign consumer inflation report cements Fed interest rate cut odds
data.description:
The final U.S. consumer inflation report of the year on Wednesday cemented expectations for another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve's monetary policy committee next week.

The headline and core consumer price index (CPI) for November came in-line with consensus estimates, according to data from the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. While the readings were benign, the headline CPI did accelerate on a M/M basis to its highest level since April.

As per the CME FedWatch tool, the odds of a 25 basis point rate cut by the Fed next week inched up to nearly 95% after the CPI report, from 89% a day ago and 78% a week ago.

"We expect the FOMC will reduce the federal funds rate by 25 bps at the conclusion of its upcoming meeting on December 18 while simultaneously emphasizing that future rate cuts will be slower-going and dependent on incoming data," Wells Fargo's Jay Bryson said.

"Data over the inter-meeting period suggest ongoing resilience within the U.S. economy. The labor market cooling remains contained while the last few inflation readings show a stubborn pace of price growth that remains about a percentage point above the central bank's 2% target," Bryson added.

See here for views on the CPI data from various experts.

For investors looking to track the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500), here are some exchange-traded funds of interest: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU).

More on the markets

  • November CPI: Second Consecutive Inflation Increase, Not Cause For Concern

data.title: ‘Stock pickers’ will be the best investment strategy in the near-term – SG 2025 Outlook
data.description:
The backdrop for “stock pickers” is the best it has been in 25 years with correlation of stock performance at all-time lows, according to Societe Generale in their 2025 Outlook report.

The S&P 500 (SP500) is up 6% since the election, “in line with our projection that the most bullish outcome for US stocks would be a ‘red sweep,’” said Manish Kabra, head of U.S. equity strategy and multi-asset strategist.

Societe Generale analysts expect the S&P 500 (SP500) to perform in line with the profit cycle, and their EPS growth forecast is at 11%.

“We expect that ‘what to buy’ will remain one of the most important elements, and we will take our cues, both positive and negative, from the potential Republican policies and the profit-cycle,” said Kabra.

Three “big positives” expected under the Trump administration are the potential for lower taxes, less regulation and lower oil prices.

Lower taxes

“We estimate that roughly half of the S&P 500 (SP500) is impacted by the manufacturing cycle but that almost 60% of the manufacturing base is outside the U.S.,” said Kabra. “The first-order impact of tax reform is likely to be a positive 2-3% lift to EPS.”

Less regulation

“The greatest impact of Trump’s deregulatory policy stance could be in the financials’ sector (XLF), where a raft of regulations could be watered down,” said Kabra. “Financials remain our key overweight, and we expect the current backdrop to be conducive to further outperformance of the higher-beta part of the sector.”

Lower oil prices

“If the tax benefits for domestic manufacturing are extended to the oil and gas (XOP) companies, it may lead to higher rig counts,” he said. “Hence, if we were to position within the oil sector (OIH), we would suggest long oil capex over the energy sector (XLE).”

On the other hand, the potential for higher tariffs and a related trade war, the continued uncertainty on inflation and Federal Reserve rate cuts, and fiscal overspending “would be likely to raise the cost of funding for the U.S. government and ultimately for U.S. corporates too,” said Kabra.

data.title: TUI AG reports Q4 results
data.description:
• TUI AG press release (OTCPK:TUIFF): Q4 Underlying EPS of €1.70
  • Revenue of €9.43B, vs. €8.47B a year ago
  • FY25 Guidance
  • Revenue to increase by 5%-10% yoy
  • Underlying EBIT to increase by 7%-10% yoy driven in particular by expectations for Summer 2025

data.title: Relay stock climbs 6% on positive breast cancer data for RLY-2608
data.description:
• Shares of Relay Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RLAY) were up 6% in midday trading Wednesday after the company reported positive updated interim data from an early-stage clinical trial for its drug RLY-2608 in the treatment of breast cancer.
  • The data showed a median progression-free survival of 11.4 months in second-line patients with PI3Kα-mutated, HR+, HER2- locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer who received RLY-2608 600mg twice daily plus fulvestrant, according to a statement.
  • The data is being presented Wednesday at the San Antonio Breast Cancer Symposium.
  • Relay said it had cash and equivalents of around $840M at the end of Q3.
